markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify embedded Monaco workers load from Handshake packaging without CDN/external server. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: MonacoWorkerOfflineFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MonacoWorkerOfflineFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: MT-235 Monaco worker offline fixture hardening implemented: missing-worker false-PASS fixture fails closed; real offline browser proof now proves editor, ts, json, css, and html Monaco worker chunks are loopback-served from built Handshake assets with no external requests; worker-bundling build scan and dependency-policy gate pass; adversarial product review found no blocking product-code issues.

evidence.summary: Shared worker-bundling scanner helper with declaration file, missing-worker negative fixture, all-worker Monaco runtime proof helper, harness proof map, and Playwright request-ledger assertions for all five bundled worker chunks.

commands.result: exit 1 before implementation; failed resolving missing worker_bundling_scan.mjs helper, proving the new scanner seam was not implemented.
exit 1 before runtime proof implementation; monacoWorkerProofs was undefined while legacy ts-worker proof still passed.
exit 0; 2 Playwright offline dependency-policy tests passed.

exit 0; tsc + vite build + build:harness succeeded; app/dist and app/dist-harness each had editor/ts/json/css/html worker chunks, no missing Monaco workers, no external worker refs, no CDN hits, and no stray writes.
exit 0; dependency-policy summary passed, including worker-bundling over existing built artifacts.

evidence.governance_note: Reviewer product findings found no blocking product-code issues. Reviewer flagged governance closeout because MT-235 was still CLAIMED and exact files/evidence were not yet recorded; this READY_FOR_VALIDATION update addresses that state/evidence gap without setting completed_by.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
